heading: Dedicated Search Nodes
body:
You can deploy [Search Nodes](what-is-search-node) to
improve performance, scalability, and resource isolation.

If you've deployed Search Nodes, consider the following:

- Adding and adjusting [shards](manual/sharding) triggers a
  rebuild of the MongoDB Search index. During this index rebuild, the index
  might not have the most current data. Therefore, queries against
  data on those shards might fail or return incorrect results.

- If you [reshard](manual/core/sharding-reshard-a-collection) a
  collection with MongoDB Search indexes, MongoDB Search indexes on the collection
  become unavailable when the resharding operation completes. You
  *must* manually rebuild the MongoDB Search indexes once the operation
  completes.

- If you issue the command to [change the primary shard](manual/reference/command/movePrimary) of a database, the MongoDB Search
  indexes for any unsharded collection under this database become
  unavailable once the operation completes. You *must* delete and
  create new MongoDB Search indexes once the [movePrimary](manual/reference/command/movePrimary)
  operation is complete.

- Scaling your cluster by adding search nodes or by changing the search
  tier triggers a rebuild of the full MongoDB Search index. However, if your cluster
  on AWS (Amazon Web Services) or Azure (Microsoft Azure) has dedicated search nodes for which you haven't
  enabled [Encryption at Rest using Customer Key Management](security-kms-encryption), Atlas provides the following
  optimizations:

  - When you scale your search nodes, Atlas uses a recent copy of
    your index in S3 (Simple Storage Service) or Azure Blob Storage instead of rebuilding the entire
    MongoDB Search index on the new node.

  - For existing nodes, Atlas periodically takes and uploads a new
    incremental list of index files. Atlas retains index files for up
    to fourteen (14) days.

  This is not yet available for clusters with dedicated search nodes on
  Google Cloud.

To learn more about MongoDB Search node architecture, see
[MongoDB Search Deployment Options](fts-deployment-options).
